notes: Reclassified to Fimbriaphyllia paraancora (Veron 1990 / 2017 revision); still widely listed under Euphyllia in trade. Wikipedia article covers genus including this species.
Despite the common name 'elephant ear anemone', Wikipedia clarifies this is a corallimorpharian (mushroom coral), not a true anemone. Predatory — can trap and consume fish.
Reclassified as Fimbriaphyllia divisa in 2017; Euphyllia divisa remains the commonly used trade name.
Commonly sold as bubble coral or grape cristata; genus Plerogyra includes Plerogyra sinuosa (standard bubble coral) and related species.
Actiniarian (true anemone); accepted per slice definition. Most popular clownfish-hosting anemone in the hobby.
No dedicated Wikipedia article for R. indosinensis; using genus Rhodactis article which lists the species. Corallimorpharian, not a true coral, but accepted per slice definition.
Octocoral (clove/star polyp); Clavularia viridis and other spp. traded as jasmine polyps or clove polyps.
January 2026 pageview spike (874 vs ~155 typical) suggests a news or social media event; 12-month mean used.
Soft coral (octocoral, Alcyoniidae). Commonly sold as Devil's Hand leather coral.
Contains palytoxin; produces one of the most potent naturally occurring toxins known. Widely sold in the reef hobby.
The "Space Invader" morph is among the most iconic named coral morphs in the hobby.
Also important in paleoclimatology; coral cores used as climate proxies.

Wikipedia disambig page; coral article is at Sarcophyton_(coral). August 2025 spike (1614 views) inflates monthly average. Covered by LiveAquaria, Bulk Reef Supply (ORA Sarcophyton Toadstool), World Wide Corals (WWC Neon Polyp Toadstool Leather), and Reef2Reef.

Wikipedia has two articles named Stylophora: the coral is at Stylophora_(coral); the other is an extinct echinoderm class. Covered by LiveAquaria, Bulk Reef Supply, World Wide Corals (WWC Blue/Purple Stylophora), and Reef2Reef.

Wikipedia pageviews show a large spike in May 2026 (2588 views); monthly average elevated as a result. Covered by LiveAquaria, Reef2Reef, Bulk Reef Supply (ORA Pom Pom Xenia), and World Wide Corals (Pulsing Xenia).
Note: Acanthastrea lordhowensis was reclassified as Micromussa lordhowensis in 2016; the genus Acanthastrea still valid for other species. BRS care guides reference both names.
Actinodiscus is a synonym of Discosoma (Corallimorpharia); Wikipedia redirects to Discosoma article. Commonly listed under Actinodiscus in aquarium trade.
Wikipedia article uses spelling 'Caulastraea' (two a's); both spellings appear in literature.
Corallimorphs, not true corals; among the most beginner-friendly reef animals.
Commonly sold under the trade name 'chalice coral', often alongside other genera (Oxypora, Mycedium).
Octocoral gorgonian; sold as purple candelabra gorgonian. BRS carries gorgonians generically but no Eunicea-specific product found.
Collection is illegal in Florida and much of the US; legally protected. Aquacultured specimens exist.
Pachyclavularia violacea is the traditional trade name; taxonomically reclassified as Briareum violaceum (family Briareidae). Wikipedia article is under Briareum violaceum.
Actiniarian (true anemone); accepted per slice definition. WWC carries a Condylactis anemone product.
Millepora is a hydrozoan, not a true scleractinian coral. Rarely available in the aquarium trade; importation restricted in many jurisdictions. Listed CITES Appendix II.
Near Threatened (IUCN); octocoral with a distinctive hard calcium carbonate skeleton. One of the few octocorals with a calcified skeleton.
Basionym: Scolymia australis; still widely sold as 'Scoly' or 'Scolymia' in the reef trade. Wikipedia genus article lists species.
Commonly sold as 'Snowflake Coral' or 'Stick/Tree Polyp'; also known as an invasive species in Hawaii.
Distichopora is a hydrozoan (family Stylasteridae), not a true scleractinian or octocoral, but is commonly marketed as 'lace coral' in the saltwater aquarium trade.
Plating/encrusting LPS often sold as chalice variant. ORA Blue Chalice Echinopora + WWC Fire Echino Chalice. Missed by LA/WWC catalog scrape; surfaced via BRS gap-fill pass.

Actiniarian (true anemone); accepted per slice definition. BRS and WWC do not appear to stock Haddon's carpet anemone; low sourceCoverage reflects specialist/uncommon availability despite high Wikipedia traffic.
Zoantharian (Zoanthidae), not a scleractinian coral. Accepted per slice definition. Sold as snake polyps. Niche availability — BRS and WWC do not appear to stock.
Fungiidae (free-living stony coral). BRS does not appear to carry Lithophyllon specifically.

Actiniarian (true anemone); accepted per slice definition. Wikipedia article is under the synonym Radianthus doreensis. BRS and WWC do not stock this species. Macrodactyla doreensis is the accepted trade name; synonyms include Radianthus doreensis.
Wikipedia article is for genus Merulina; M. ampliata is the type species.
Acanthastrea lordhowensis was reclassified as Micromussa lordhowensis in 2016; many specimens are still sold under the 'Acan' name.
Wikipedia's 'Nephthea' article redirects to Litophyton following taxonomic revision; genus Nephthea s.s. is still used in the aquarium trade.
Wikipedia article is for genus Swiftia; S. exserta is listed in the species section. NPS (non-photosynthetic); expert-level care.
Actinarian (true anemone), not a coral; taxon scope accepts anemones. Wikipedia page is for genus Cryptodendrum.
IUCN Vulnerable; anemone-like appearance with long tentacles. May 2026 pageview spike (1,098) likely skews the mean upward.
Fungia now contains only a single extant species (F. fungites); what the hobby calls 'Fungia' often encompasses Cycloseris and other Fungiidae genera.
Reef aquarium specimens frequently mislabeled as Platygyra are often Paragoniastrea australiensis.
Reef2Reef wiki returned 403 for all search URLs. Wikipedia stub article. General gorgonian care discussed extensively on R2R.
No dedicated Reef2Reef wiki or BRS product page found. Wikipedia used as secondarySourceUrl.
Scientific name Heteractis magnifica is now a synonym; current accepted name is Radianthus magnifica per Wikipedia and WoRMS.

Candidate scientificName Epicystis crucifer is a synonym; Wikipedia article is under current accepted name Phymanthus crucifer. Well discussed on reef forums though no dedicated Reef2Reef wiki page found.
Reef2Reef discusses 'rusty gorgonian' often attributed to Muricea elongata; genus Muricella is confirmed correct per LiveAquaria listing.
BRS mentions sun coral briefly in NPS soft coral article. WWC does not appear to carry Tubastraea. Wikipedia secondarySourceUrl used as best available specialty page.
No dedicated species Wikipedia article for Rhodactis yuma; using genus article Rhodactis. Frequently discussed alongside Ricordea yuma on reef forums.
LPS sold as chalice variant. ORA Easter Egg Chalice Coral explicitly labeled Oxypora sp. Missed by LA/WWC; surfaced via BRS gap-fill pass.

Protopalythoa pageviews are low as it lacks a dedicated Wikipedia article; redirected to Palythoa for scoring. Taxonomic boundary with Palythoa is disputed; many hobby 'button polyps' are Protopalythoa.
Wikipedia article is for genus Muriceopsis; M. flavida is listed with image. No dedicated BRS/WWC page found.
No Wikipedia article for species Diodogorgia nodulifera; genus article Diodogorgia exists with very low traffic (4 months of data, avg ~47). Species confirmed on Reef2Reef with dedicated thread.

Scientific name Heteractis crispa is a synonym; current accepted name is Radianthus crispa. Wikipedia pageviews very low (avg 38/month), suggesting article is not widely visited despite species being common in the trade.
Azooxanthellate deep-sea cup coral. Reef2Reef confirms Cladopsammia gracilis is kept in hobby and is similar to Dendrophyllia.

Wellsophyllia radiata is a junior synonym of Trachyphyllia geoffroyi (confirmed by Wikipedia and Reef2Reef). Hobbyist name 'Wellso' persists in the trade. Wikipedia article title is 'Open brain coral' (Trachyphyllia geoffroyi).
ORA-aquacultured encrusting soft coral. Niche genus not surfaced by LiveAquaria/WWC catalog scrape; added via BRS gap-fill pass.
Photosynthetic gorgonian. ORA-aquacultured. Niche genus not surfaced by LA/WWC; surfaced via BRS gap-fill pass.
No standalone Wikipedia article. Genetic studies suggest A. deshayesiana is synonymous with Cynarina lacrymalis; name still widely used in aquarium trade. Strong commercial presence (WWC, Reef2Reef).

No Wikipedia article for the genus. 'Blueberry sea fan' name applied to Acalycigorgia sp. and sometimes Acanthogorgia sp. (formerly reclassified). Expert-only care due to non-photosynthetic feeding requirements.

No English Wikipedia article exists for Nemenzophyllia turbida. Score is 0 per formula (no Wikipedia URL). Confirmed by R2R and WWC. Niche LPS with reported high mortality; decreasing availability in the hobby.
No dedicated Wikipedia article for Mycedium genus. Previously in family Pectiniidae, now placed in Merulinidae following 2012 revision. Well-established in the hobby as a chalice coral.
No dedicated Wikipedia article found for Pterogorgia. Caribbean gorgonian genus; photosynthetic; P. anceps and P. citrina are common in the trade.
No Wikipedia article found for Stylocoeniella. Confirmed by WWC (primary) and Reef2Reef with dedicated coral spotlight threads. Niche SPS encrusting coral with limited mainstream coverage.
